China Development Bank Financial Lease Agreement with Qinghai Huangnan Yuedian New Energy Co., Ltd
data.description:
China Development Bank Financial Leasing Co., Ltd. announced that on 27 November 2025 (after trading hours), the Company (as the Lessor) entered into the Finance Lease Agreement with the Lessee, pursuant to which, (i) the Lessor agreed to purchase the Leased Assets from the Lessee at a consideration of RMB 1,220,000,000, and (ii) the Lessor agreed to lease the Leased Assets to the Lessee with a lease period of 192 months. According to Chapter 14 of the Listing Rules, as the highest applicable percentage ratio of the transaction under the Finance Lease Agreement is higher than 5% but lower than 25%, the transaction constitutes a discloseable transaction of the Company and is subject to the reporting and announcement requirements but is exempt from the circular and shareholders' approval requirements under Chapter 14 of the Listing rules. The Board hereby announces that on 27November 2025 (after trading hours), The Company (as the Lessor") entered into the Finance Lease agreement with the Lessee, pursuant To which, (i) theLessor agreed to purchase the Lease Assets from the Lessee at an consideration of RMB 1, 220,000,000, and(ii) the Lessor agreed To the best of the Directors' knowledge, information and belief after having made all reasonable enquiries, the Lessor and its ultimate beneficial owner are both independent third parties of the Company and its connected persons (as defined in the Listing Rules).

The Leased Assets are the photovoltaic power station facilities located in Qinghai Province, the PRC. The net book value of the Leased Assets is approximately RMB 1,500,000,000 in aggregate. The Lessee does not separately calculate the profits before and after tax of the Leased Assets.

The terms of the Finance Lease Agreement, including the Leased Assets' transfer consideration, lease principal, lease interest and other expenses under the Finance Lease Agreement, were determined upon arm's length negotiation between the Lessee and the Lessor with reference to the net book value of the Lease Assets and the prevailing market prices of the same category of finance lease products in the PRC. Leased Assets and Their Ownership. The Lessee has agreed to transfer and/or change the registration of the Leased Assets to theLessor during the lease period.

The total transfer consideration is RMB 1,220,000,000, which shall be paid by the Lessor's self-owned funds and/or commercial loans. At the same time, the Lessor has agreed to lease the Leased assets to the Lessee. The Lessee is entitled to the possession, usage and benefits of such assets.

Upon expiration of the lease period, the Lessee may repurchase the Leased assets from the Lessor at a consideration of RMB 100 in nominal value. Guarantee. Qinghai Zhongren Construction Co., Ltd. (Qing Hai Zhongren Construction Co.

Ltd) and Mr. LI Gaochen (Li Gao Chen) as its ultimate beneficial owner undertake joint and several liability guarantee in respect of the debt commitment by the Lessee under the Finance Lease Agreement.

Leonardo Unveils Michelangelo Dome Defense System - The Security Dome
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Leonardo Spa on Thursday introduced Michelangelo Dome, the company's new advanced integrated defense system designed to address emerging threats in an increasingly complex global landscape.

As explained by the company in a statement, the project was developed in response to the need to protect critical infrastructure, sensitive urban areas, territories, and assets of national and European interest. The system offers a modular, open, scalable, and multi-domain solution, aligning with Leonardo's broader strategy to strengthen its position as a leading player in the field of global security.

"Michelangelo Dome is not a single system, but a comprehensive architecture that integrates next-generation ground, naval, aerial, and space sensors, cyber defense platforms, command and control systems, artificial intelligence, and coordinated effectors," the statement reads.

Leonardo shares rose by 1.2% to EUR46.13 per share.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: US SEC probing Jefferies for bankrupt First Brands dealings, FT reports
data.description:
(Reuters) -The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is probing investment bank Jefferies over its relationship with bankrupt auto parts supplier First Brands Group, the Financial Times reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The SEC is seeking information from Jefferies about whether it gave investors in one of its funds enough information about their exposure to the auto business, the report said.

Jefferies said in October that it had limited exposure to First Brands, which filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection in September, and said any potential losses would be "readily absorbable."  

Jefferies' Leucadia Asset Management fund, through its credit fund Point Bonita, held about $715 million in receivables linked to First Brands. The SEC is investigating whether Point Bonita's investors were aware of the relationship, according to the FT report.

Jefferies declined to comment. Both First Brands and the SEC did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

UBS said earlier this month it was winding down investment funds run by its hedge fund unit O'Connor, after suffering losses due to exposure to First Brands.

(Reporting by Prerna Bedi in Bengaluru; Editing by Leslie Adler)

data.description:

The Board of Directors announced that, at the Board of Directors meeting of the Company held immediately following the EGM, the Board of Directors resolved to adjust the

Eni inaugurates gas treatment plant in Soyo, Angola
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Eni Spa, through its subsidiary Azule Energy - a 50% joint venture with bp and operator of the New Gas Consortium - inaugurated the NGC gas processing plant in Soyo, northern Angola, on Thursday.

The NGC project, operated by Eni prior to the establishment of Azule Energy, is the first non-associated gas development in Angola and has a treatment capacity of approximately 400 million standard cubic feet of gas per day and 20,000 barrels of oil per day.

The gas, which comes from the offshore fields of Quiluma and Maboqueiro, is treated and then supplied to the Angola LNG plant for export and domestic consumption, as explained by the company in a statement.

Eni is down 0.4% to EUR15.92 per share.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Milan Prosecutor Investigates MPS Takeover Bid for Mediobanca
data.description:
Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone, Delfin chairman Francesco Milleri, and Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena (MPS) CEO Luigi Lovaglio are under investigation for market manipulation and obstruction of supervisory authorities in relation to MPS's takeover attempt of Mediobanca.

Judicial sources confirmed the probe, as previously reported by Corriere della Sera.

MPS, in an official statement, acknowledged that Lovaglio has received a notice of investigation and that the bank has been searched by authorities. The bank expressed confidence in its ability to clarify its conduct and pledged full cooperation with investigators.

Following the news, MPS shares saw heavy selling, plummeting by over 7% before recouping some losses. As of 16:40, MPS was down 4.16% at EUR8.365, while Mediobanca lost 2.69% after earlier dropping nearly 5%.

According to the Prosecutor's Office, supervisory authorities Consob, the European Central Bank (ECB), and Ivass were allegedly kept in the dark about a coordinated action involving MPS and its two main shareholders to acquire Mediobanca, sources said.

Lovaglio, Caltagirone, and Milleri have reportedly been under investigation since June, but their names have surfaced only now after magistrates Roberto Pellicano, Luca Gaglio, and Giovanni Polizzi launched searches as part of the ongoing inquiry.

A representative for Milleri declined to comment, and Caltagirone's representative could not be reached.

Last September, MPS, led by Lovaglio since early 2022, completed its public exchange offer for Mediobanca.

MPS's largest shareholders are Caltagirone, a construction sector entrepreneur, and Delfin, the holding company of the Del Vecchio family, headed by EssilorLuxottica CEO Milleri.

MPS announced its bid for Mediobanca in January, following the Treasury's completion of the re-privatization of the rescued bank, reducing its stake from 68% to about 12%. That stake was further halved with the Mediobanca operation.

Caltagirone and Delfin were also major shareholders of Mediobanca and played a key role in backing the offer.

(Reporting from Milan by Sara Rossi, editing by Andrea Mandalà)

data.title: ICE Canola Futures Ease Off
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were lower on Thursday morning despite strength from comparable oils.

European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were higher, while crude oil was up slightly as Russia-Ukraine ceasefire talks outweighed rising U.S. supplies. Chicago soyoil wasn't traded due to the U.S. Thanksgiving day holiday. All other U.S. markets were also closed today.

The Canadian dollar was steady compared with Wednesday's close.

Nearly 7,500 contracts were traded. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric ton: 


 
Canola    Price    Change 
Jan      647.20   dn 4.50 
Mar      660.20   dn 4.80 
May      670.30   dn 5.20 
Jul      675.50   dn 5.30

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-27-25 1125ET

data.title: Brazil seeks US cooperation in money laundering probe
Money for transport projects ready for construction should flow quickly
data.description:
BERLIN (dpa-AFX) - Transport Minister Patrick Schnieder wants to make money available in the short term for new construction and expansion projects in the motorway network that are ready for construction. "Once the budget has been passed, we will approve projects that are ready for construction next week," the CDU politician assured during the budget deliberations in the Bundestag. This is "an important signal" for both the construction industry and the citizens affected.

On Friday, parliament will vote on the federal budget for the coming year. At the beginning of the deliberations, the Ministry of Transport had warned of financial shortfalls amounting to billions in the coming years. As a result, the leaders of the black-red coalition decided at the beginning of October that all construction-ready transport projects would also be built. Subsequently, the budget for Autobahn GmbH was increased once again by the Bundestag's budget committee. /ax/DP/mis

Italy's MPS faces investigation over Mediobanca takeover
data.description:
MILAN (Reuters) -Italy's Monte dei Paschi di Siena (MPS) and its top two shareholders are facing an investigation in Milan in relation to the Tuscan bank's takeover of Mediobanca, two judicial sources told Reuters on Thursday.

MPS, which has been led by Luigi Lovaglio since early 2022, confirmed it had been informed of allegations against its 70-year-old chief executive and of a search warrant and was cooperating with the authorities.

"The bank is confident it can provide all the necessary information to clarify the correctness of its actions and expresses its full trust in the competent authorities, with whom it confirms its full cooperation," MPS said in a statement.

The 16 billion euro ($19 billion) bid which in September handed MPS control of Milanese investment bank Mediobanca was championed by the government and is the biggest deal so far in a merger wave reshaping Italy's financial landscape.

Milan prosecutors allege Italy's market watchdog Consob, the European Central Bank (ECB) and Italian insurance supervisor IVASS were not informed of concerted action involving MPS and its leading investors on the Mediobanca bid, the sources said.

They are investigating possible market rigging and obstruction of regulators, the two sources said, confirming a report in Italian newspaper Corriere della Sera.

Shares in MPS, which was bailed out by the state in 2017, were down 5% at 1508 GMT.

The ECB declined to comment. Consob Chair Paolo Savona said the regulator had to look into the matter before commenting.

The biggest shareholders in MPS are Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone, a construction magnate, and Delfin, the holding company of Italy's Del Vecchio family, which is led by EssilorLuxottica CEO Francesco Milleri, the right-hand man of late billionaire Leonardo Del Vecchio.

Caltagirone and Delfin were also the top investors in Mediobanca and played a key role in supporting the MPS bid.

They became shareholders in MPS in November 2024 when Italy's Treasury sold 15% of the bank to a small group of domestic investors. They later increased their holdings, easing the takeover project.

UNDER INVESTIGATION SINCE JUNE

Lovaglio, Caltagirone and Milleri have all been under investigation since June, the sources told Reuters, but their names have only emerged after prosecutors began searches in connection with their investigation.

A representative for Milleri declined to comment. It was not immediately possible to reach a representative for Caltagirone.

The offices of MPS have been searched this week, said the sources, who asked not to be named.

The Milan prosecutors in charge of the case are Roberto Pellicano, Luca Gaglio and Giovanni Polizzi.

MPS announced its bid for Mediobanca in January after Italy's Treasury completed its re-privatisation of the bailed-out bank, cutting its initial 68% stake to a near 12% holding which has now more than halved with the Mediobanca deal.

($1 = 0.8631 euros)

(Reporting by Alvise Armellini, Gianluca Semeraro and Angelo Amante, editing by Alvise Armellini, Valentina Za, Gavin Jones and Alexander Smith)

By Valentina Za and Alvise Armellini

Mowi issues green bonds worth NOK 4.5 billion
data.description:
Norwegian salmon farming company Mowi has issued green bonds totaling NOK 4.5 billion. This is according to a press release.

The issue consists of two parts: bonds worth NOK 3.5 billion with a five-year maturity and an interest rate of three-month Nibor plus 110 basis points, and bonds worth NOK 1 billion with a five-year maturity and a fixed interest rate of 4.95 percent.

data.title: JPMorgan European Growth & Income outperforms benchmark at half year
data.description:
(Alliance News) - JPMorgan European Growth & Income PLC on Thursday noted its position as the "best performing investment trust in its sector", as it reported outperformance against its peers and benchmark during the interim period.

The London-based investment fund reported a total return on net assets per share of 14.2% for the six months that ended September 30, beating its benchmark, the MSCI Europe ex UK, which returned 10.7% over the same period.

The trust's net asset value per share grew 12.1% to 132.4 pence from 118.1p at March 31.

Chair Rita Dhut attributed stock selection as the primary reason for the period of outperformance.

The trust cited Heidelberg Materials AG as one of the main contributors to its strong performance, as it noted a "much more price rational" cement market owing to regulatory changes.

On the other hand, Carlsberg AS served as the main detractor as it printed weaker than anticipated volumes, particularly in Asia, said the company.

The trust noted that the company was voted the best investment company in the European sector at the AIC Investment Week Award ceremony held on Wednesday last week.

JPMorgan European Growth & Income reported an interim dividend of 2.4p per share, flat with the prior year.

Its shares were trading down 0.7% at 134.00 pence on Thursday afternoon in London.

Looking ahead, Portfolio Managers Alexander Fitzalan Howard, Zenah Shuhaiber and Tim Lewis said European valuations "remain attractive", noting that investment flows into these markets have "started to pick up".

They noted that they "continue to find stock picking opportunities that meet our criteria regarding valuation, quality and operational momentum."

By Christopher Ward, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com
